hero.title: Sustainable Development through Local Value Creation in Africa
hero.subtitle: We promote climate-resilient livelihoods, food security and nature conservation through integrated, community-led projects

about.title: About Mimea Impact
about.intro: Mimea Impact is a Swiss non-profit association founded in 2026, dedicated to sustainable development in East Africa. We believe in an integrated approach that combines community development, food security, nature conservation, and climate resilience.
about.introText: Our work is guided by rigorous scientific principles, strong local ownership, and complete transparency. We collaborate closely with local communities, academic institutions, and partner organizations to create sustainable and measurable impact.
about.missionText: Our mission is to support local entrepreneurship and livelihoods through activities related to moringa and other local plant crops. We combine these economic opportunities with essential environmental objectives: biodiversity conservation and reducing conflicts between communities and wild animals.
about.boardIntro: Our board brings diverse expertise in governance, law, and international project management.
about.teamIntro: Our field team combines academic expertise with deep local knowledge for effective and culturally adapted implementation.

teamMember1.name: Fabrice Volken, MSc ETH
teamMember1.bio: Fabrice is the founder of Paperwork AG and the non-profit foundation Büro-Spitex. He has always combined entrepreneurial action with social commitment. Currently active in botanicals and natural cosmetics, he brings directly relevant expertise to the Moringa programme.

teamMember2.name: Esther Mrabu
teamMember2.bio: Esther Mrabu has 14 years of experience in Kilifi County, including organic Moringa farming, outgrower support and microfinance training. She leads the Vuka Mama Initiative ('Stand up, Mother' in Swahili), a women-led organisation that managed on-the-ground Moringa production under the World Bank KEMFSED project.

about.legalText: Association under Art. 60 ff. Swiss Civil Code | Headquarters: Adliswil, Zurich | Non-profit recognition pending

lumoDetail.title: Lumo+ | Conservation Tourism & Community Development
lumoDetail.challengeTitle: The Challenge: Transforming Conflicts into Opportunities
lumoDetail.challengePara1: In Kenya, communities living near conservation areas bear the high costs of coexistence with wildlife: crop destruction, threats to livestock, risks to human safety. Yet they rarely benefit from the revenue generated by conservation tourism.
lumoDetail.challengePara2: This economic injustice creates growing tensions between conservation and community development. Without viable economic alternatives, communities lose their motivation to protect wildlife, threatening Kenya's unique ecosystems.
lumoDetail.challengeBoxText: This project demonstrates that well-designed conservation tourism can create sustainable jobs, generate significant local revenue, and transform the perception of wildlife from "costly threat" to "valuable economic asset".
lumoDetail.approachTitle: Our Approach: A Sustainable Business Model
lumoDetail.approachSubtitle: Unlike traditional projects depending on continuous grants, Lumo+ establishes a self-sustaining business model based on three complementary pillars.
lumoDetail.approachHighlight: Each pillar generates revenue from year one and creates sustainable local jobs
lumoDetail.pillar1Desc: Professional safari operations led by an experienced and award-winning ranger. Generates immediate revenue and creates 15-20 skilled jobs (guides, drivers, reception staff).
lumoDetail.pillar1Benefits: ✓ Profitable from year 1 • ✓ Immediate direct jobs
lumoDetail.pillar2Desc: Low environmental impact accommodation infrastructure: renewable energy, rainwater harvesting, zero plastic. Significantly increases revenue capacity.
lumoDetail.pillar2Benefits: ✓ High environmental standards • ✓ Attracts premium tourism
lumoDetail.pillar3Desc: Agricultural diversification (drought-resistant sunflower), community bakery and beekeeping. Creates 30-40 additional jobs and improves local food security.
lumoDetail.pillar3Benefits: ✓ Stable agricultural income • ✓ Enhanced food security
lumoDetail.impactIntro: Lumo+ aims for complete economic transformation of the community conservancy in 4 years, with concrete and measurable results at each stage.
lumoDetail.impactJobsDetail: 15-20 in safaris, 10-15 at the lodge, 25-30 in agriculture
lumoDetail.impactRevenueDetail: From CHF 20'000 to CHF 180'000 annually for the community
lumoDetail.impact1Text: The project becomes financially independent after 4 years, eliminating dependence on donations
lumoDetail.impact2Text: Communities perceive wildlife as an economic asset, reducing conflicts
lumoDetail.impact3Text: Training in tourism management, professional guiding and agricultural entrepreneurship
lumoDetail.impact4Text: Creates a blueprint for other community conservancies in Kenya
lumoDetail.ctaText: Your support enables the creation of sustainable local jobs and promotes harmonious coexistence between communities and wildlife.

moringaDetail.title: Moringa Circular Economy | Livelihoods Programme
moringaDetail.challengePara1: Kilifi County on Kenya's coast is among the country's regions most severely affected by food insecurity. Over 30% of children under five suffer from chronic malnutrition with acute deficiencies in iron, zinc and vitamin A. Around 47.5% of women live below the poverty line, compared to 36.1% nationally.
moringaDetail.challengePara2: Widespread charcoal production drives deforestation and threatens fragile coastal ecosystems. Smallholder farmers sell their raw products at rock-bottom prices to middlemen, and the absence of local processing infrastructure keeps them at the bottom of the value chain.
moringaDetail.challengeBoxText: Moringa oleifera is one of the most nutrient-dense plants on earth and thrives in Kilifi's tropical climate. Our circular economy model ensures every part of the Moringa tree is used: leaves for nutrition, seeds for oil, byproducts as animal feed and organic fertiliser, waste for biogas production.
moringaDetail.approachTitle: Our Solution: The Moringa Circular Economy
moringaDetail.approachSubtitle: The programme establishes a community processing centre where Moringa is transformed into multiple high-value products. The central innovation is the circular economy: every byproduct of one process becomes the input for the next.
moringaDetail.approachHighlight: Proven model: These packages were developed, tested and successfully implemented in Kilifi County under the same World Bank programme (KEMFSED).
moringaDetail.packagesSubtitle: Each package can be funded individually or as part of the whole. Packages 1 (Nutrition), 2 (Body care) and 5 (Organic) deliver impact on their own. Packages 3 (Feed) and 4 (Bakery) reach their full circular potential when the shared processing infrastructure is in place.
moringaDetail.package1Desc: Moringa leaf powder is added to maize flour (Ugali) and distributed via schools, health stations and community health workers. Targets 3 CIGs (~75 households), reaching 200+ households through existing health infrastructure. Goal: reduce anaemia prevalence by 20% within 18 months.
moringaDetail.package2Desc: A women-led production workshop for Moringa-based soaps, body oils, balms and natural cosmetics. 40 women are trained in oil extraction, soap making, quality control, packaging and business management. Target: KES 5,000-10,000 additional monthly income per participant.
moringaDetail.package3Desc: Processing byproducts (~2,800 kg in year 1, over 5,000 kg from year 3) are transformed into protein-rich animal feed pellets mixed with locally available coconut and cassava waste. Reduces feed costs by 30-40% compared to imported concentrates.
moringaDetail.package4Desc: A community bakery producing Moringa-enriched bread, Mandazi and Chapati, powered by biogas from the processing centre. Run by a self-help group of 30 women. Estimated CO2 reduction of 2-4 tonnes per year by replacing charcoal.
moringaDetail.package5Desc: Training smallholder farmers in organic farming methods and preparing them for group organic certification via an Internal Control System (ICS). 50 farmers across 3 CIGs. Enables a 15-25% price premium for certified organic Moringa.
moringaDetail.packageMgmtDesc: On-site programme coordination, monitoring and evaluation, financial reporting and quality assurance. The project leader is regularly present in Kilifi County for hands-on management.
moringaDetail.impactIntro: The programme reaches an estimated 150 households (approximately 750 people) and 70 women directly. Over 400 additional households benefit indirectly through nutrition distribution, market access and knowledge transfer.
moringaDetail.impactDirectDetail: 150 households across multiple CIGs and self-help groups
moringaDetail.impactWomenDetail: In production, training and business management
moringaDetail.impactIndirectDetail: Through nutrition sachets, markets and knowledge transfer
moringaDetail.impact1Text: Reliable supply of affordable, nutrient-rich supplements for children and mothers. Target: 20% reduction in anaemia prevalence within 18 months.
moringaDetail.impact2Text: 40 women trained in production and business management with sustainable income from Moringa body care products and bakery operations.
moringaDetail.impact3Text: All Moringa byproducts converted into valuable feed, fertiliser and biogas. Feed costs reduced by 30-40% for local livestock farmers.
moringaDetail.impact4Text: Biogas bakery replaces charcoal, reducing CO2 by 2-4 tonnes per year. Organic farming methods improve soil health and reduce chemical inputs.
moringaDetail.sustainabilityIntro: The processing centre is designed to cover its operating costs from year 3 onwards through sales of enriched maize flour, animal feed, body care products and baked goods.
moringaDetail.partner1Desc: Women-led Kenyan community organisation with extensive experience from the World Bank KEMFSED project. Implementation partner on the ground in Kilifi County.
moringaDetail.partner2Desc: Academic partner providing scientific support, quality assurance and knowledge transfer for the Moringa programme.
moringaDetail.ctaText: With CHF 15,000 you can fund the complete bakery equipment (oven, biogas connection, training for 30 women) or the full organic certification process for 50 farmers.

legalPage.title: Legal Notice
legalPage.legalFormText: Association under Art. 60 ff. of the Swiss Civil Code (ZGB). Non-profit recognition application pending with the Canton of Zurich Tax Office.
legalPage.liabilityText: Mimea Impact makes every effort to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the information on this website. However, we accept no liability for the timeliness, correctness, or completeness of the content. Links to third-party websites are provided for convenience only; we have no control over their content and accept no responsibility for it.
legalPage.copyrightText: All content on this website (text, images, graphics, logos) is the property of Mimea Impact unless otherwise stated. Any reproduction or use requires prior written consent.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.generalText: We take the protection of your personal data seriously. This website is operated in accordance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nDSG) and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
privacyPage.dataCollectedText: We only collect personal data that you voluntarily provide to us:
privacyPage.dataContact: Contact form: name, email address, and your message. This data is used solely to respond to your enquiry.
privacyPage.dataNewsletter: Newsletter: your email address, used exclusively to send you project updates. You can unsubscribe at any time.
privacyPage.cookiesText: This website uses only technically necessary cookies (e.g. language preference). We do not use tracking cookies, analytics tools, or advertising technologies.
privacyPage.thirdPartiesText: This website is hosted by Infomaniak (Geneva, Switzerland). Infomaniak may process server log data (IP address, browser type, access time) for security purposes. No other third-party services are integrated.
privacyPage.rightsText: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data at any time. Please contact us at info@mimea-impact.ch.
privacyPage.lawText: Swiss law applies. The place of jurisdiction is Adliswil, Canton of Zurich.

statutesPage.title: Statutes
statutesPage.intro: The following is a summary of the key articles from the statutes of Mimea Impact, adopted at the founding assembly. The full statutes are available upon request.
statutesPage.nameText: Under the name "Mimea Impact", an association is constituted in accordance with Art. 60 ff. of the Swiss Civil Code (ZGB).
statutesPage.seatText: The registered office of the association is in Adliswil, Canton of Zurich.
statutesPage.purposeText: The association pursues non-profit and charitable purposes. It promotes sustainable development in East Africa, particularly through local value creation, food security, women's empowerment, and environmental conservation.
statutesPage.membershipText: Any natural or legal person who supports the purpose of the association may become a member. Admission is decided by the Board.
statutesPage.organsText: The governing bodies of the association are: the General Assembly, the Board, and the Auditors.
statutesPage.assemblyText: The General Assembly is the supreme body of the association. It meets at least once per year and decides on statutes amendments, the budget, annual accounts, and election of Board members.
statutesPage.boardText: The Board consists of at least three members elected by the General Assembly. It manages the association's affairs, represents it externally, and can delegate operational tasks to a project team.
statutesPage.financesText: The association's resources consist of membership fees, donations, grants, and income from its activities. Board members serve on a voluntary basis.
statutesPage.dissolutionText: In the event of dissolution, the association's assets shall be transferred to a tax-exempt organisation with a similar purpose. Distribution among members is excluded.
statutesPage.fullVersion: The complete statutes are available upon request at info@mimea-impact.ch.

footer.description: Sustainable development organization working for the resilience of rural populations in East Africa.
